title: CORE Lemma Record
description: A single lemma entry in a CORE language pack. Lemmas are the stable lexical unit — not tokens, not surface forms.

lemma_id.description: Unique identifier in the form '<lang>:<canonical_form>'. Example: 'he:dabar'.

language.description: ISO 639-1 code for the pack language.

script_form.description: The lemma in its native script. Hebrew right-to-left; Greek polytonic or monotonic as appropriate.

transliteration.description: SBL-style transliteration for Hebrew and Greek. Null for English.

gloss_seed.description: A single-word English gloss for orientation only. Not a definition. Not used in semantic lift.

pos.description: Part of speech. Examples: verb, noun, adjective, particle, conjunction, preposition.

morph_class.description: Morphological class within the pack. Hebrew: qal, niphal, etc. Greek: thematic, athematic, etc. English: regular, irregular, copular, etc.

semantic_family.description: The broad semantic domain this lemma inhabits. Must correspond to a recognized CORE field primitive family.

field_hooks.description: List of CORE field primitive targets this lemma can lift into. Must be non-empty for any lemma that participates in semantic lift.

readback_priority.description: Relative priority when this lemma is a candidate during readback disambiguation. Higher is preferred.
